meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Scale Your AI Apps Like a Pro
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with scaling issues. Learn to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to build enterprise-grade AI applications effortlessly.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template solves scaling bottlenecks by enforcing a modular, decoupled architecture that separates business logic from AI orchestration. By integrating this with Claude Code, you automate boilerplate generation and refactoring, allowing you to focus on high-level feature development instead of fighting your codebase's limitations.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: the initial prototype works, the demo is flawless, but the moment you try to add a second feature, the entire architecture collapses under its own weight. This is the 'Scaling Wall'—the graveyard of countless student projects that never make it to production.

But what if you could bypass the spaghetti code phase entirely? By shifting to the Antigravity project template, you stop building scripts and start architecting systems. This isn't just about cleaner code; it’s about building a robust foundation that anticipates growth before you even write the first line of logic.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the project structure that enforces strict separation of concerns, preventing the 'all-in-one-file' trap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project structure using the Antigravity template, ensuring a clear separation between /services, /api, and /core directories.

steps.title: Define Domain Models
steps.description: Use the template to strictly define your data schemas, ensuring your AI logic has a predictable structure to operate on.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate type-safe domain models for my user-content system following the Antigravity DDD pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate domain models for user content'

steps.title: Implement Modular AI Services
steps.description: Encapsulate your AI calls within dedicated service modules to allow for easy swapping of models or providers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an AI service interface that abstracts the provider logic, allowing for seamless switching between GPT-4 and Claude 3.5.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create abstract AI provider interface'

steps.title: Establish Event-Driven Communication
steps.description: Decouple your services using an event bus, ensuring that one failing component doesn't bring down your entire application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement an event emitter pattern within the Antigravity core to handle asynchronous task processing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add event-driven architecture to core services'

steps.title: Automate Testing and CI/CD
steps.description: Leverage the template's built-in testing hooks to ensure every new feature maintains the integrity of the system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the Antigravity test suite to run regression tests on all core services upon commit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure CI pipeline for regression testing'

pro_tips: Always define your interfaces before implementation; it forces you to think about the 'what' rather than the 'how'.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command on every pull request to catch architectural drift before it becomes technical debt.
Keep your AI prompts as external configuration files rather than hardcoded strings for easier version control.
Implement a 'circuit breaker' pattern in your service layer to gracefully handle API timeouts or rate limits.
Treat your project template like a product; update it every time you learn a new design pattern.
content.next_steps: Once your base architecture is live, begin migrating your existing scripts into the new service modules one by one, testing thoroughly after each module is integrated.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for today and start building for the future. Master the architecture that separates the hobbyists from the professional AI engineers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
